item.title: darkhttpd : you'll love static
item.description: darkhttpd - wiki is my favorite web server. It does one thing and it does it good: serve static files correctly. The overall best feature I’ve found in darkhttpd is that it is configured via command line arguments, so you can wrap the configuration of each one of your web servers into it’s runit/OpenRC service! Running a static webserver will help you reduce overhead and lets you reduce the resource usage of your services.
item.content:
darkhttpd - wiki is my favorite web server. It does one thing and it does it good: serve static files correctly.

The overall best feature I’ve found in darkhttpd is that it is configured via command line arguments, so you can wrap the configuration of each one of your web servers into it’s runit/OpenRC service!

Running a static webserver will help you reduce overhead and lets you reduce the resource usage of your services.

You can easily install it by running:

In Void Linux:

# xbps-install -S darkhttpd

In Alpine Linux:

# apk add darkhttpd

item.title: void-rice
item.description:
Hello world. I’ve decided to upload my suckless tools to github, so here’s void-rice. It can be barely called a desktop environment, but it features most things you need after a fresh install of Void Linux.
I’ve been using this setup for the last months and I’m really pleased by the native multihead (monitor) support with xrandr. void-rice features a custom patched version of the dwm window manager with system tray support and sane keybindings, dmenu, slstatus for the statusbar info and a patched st terminal emulator.
item.content:
Hello world.

I’ve decided to upload my suckless tools to github, so here’s void-rice. It can be barely called a desktop environment, but it features most things you need after a fresh install of Void Linux.

I’ve been using this setup for the last months and I’m really pleased by the native multihead (monitor) support with xrandr. void-rice features a custom patched version of the dwm window manager with system tray support and sane keybindings, dmenu, slstatus for the statusbar info and a patched st terminal emulator. It also includes multiple GTK themes and sane dependencies.

Installing

The automated install script is made for Void Linux but void-rice can be built on most linux distributions by installing the corresponding dependencies and manually building and installing each suckless tool.

NOTE: If the network info is not displayed correctly in the status bar you may need to replace wlan0 with your network interface in software/slstatus/config.h, rebuild slstatus and restart dwm.

The build.sh script just runs make clean && make under each program’s directory, while install.sh just runs make install as root.

I will probably remove vpm from the next release to make the install.sh script portable, and I’m thinking about packaging this as a Void Linux metapackage.

Customizing

Simply edit config.h in each program’s directory.

Contributing

Feel free to open PRs if you have any improvement in mind!

item.title: Learning Haskell by Writing a Scheme
